title: Scope service DB credentials per ControlPlane and database
description:
# GitHub Issue #435: feat(c5c3-operator): scope service database credentials per ControlPlane and per database (drop the shared flat OpenBao path)

**Description:**

Today every Keystone instance draws its MariaDB user password from one shared, flat OpenBao secret. The Keystone DB password lives at the unscoped KV-v2 path `openstack/keystone/db`, materialised into a single Kubernetes Secret named `keystone-db` by one static `ExternalSecret` (`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml`, namespace `openstack`). Both the MariaDB `User` CR (`buildUser`, `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go`) and the derived connection string (`reconcileDBConnectionSecret`, `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go`) read that Secret through `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef`. In a multi-tenant deployment — one operator fleet managing many ControlPlanes — every tenant's Keystone resolves to the *same* source secret, so rotating or compromising it hits every Keystone at once. This breaks the per-CR isolation the rest of the secret layout already enforces, and it is exactly the `Kubernetes Secrets Management` / `Secure by Design and Default` failure mode: a shared-blast-radius credential where a per-tenant one is cheap.

This issue brings service DB credentials into line with the per-ControlPlane scoping CC-0112 already established for the admin credential, and fixes the path layout so it also covers multiple databases per ControlPlane later. **Scope is the per-ControlPlane *static* KV credential only** — the near-term, mechanical migration (formerly "stage a"). The dynamic DB secret engine (short-lived, auto-revoked credentials, formerly "stage b") is the longer-term target and is **split out into #439**; it is neither designed nor implemented here.

This work lands in the **c5c3-operator**, scoping by **ControlPlane namespace + name**, mirroring the admin Application Credential (`adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor`, `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go:62`). The closest *implementation* precedent now exists: CC-0114 added `ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret` (`reconcile_korc.go:1190`), the operator-created, per-ControlPlane, owner-ref'd `ExternalSecret` that reads one of these per-CR OpenBao paths from `openbao-cluster-store` and materialises it into `childNamespace(cp)` — this issue's `dbCredentialExternalSecret(cp)` builder is structurally the same. The Keystone operator is unchanged: it keeps consuming `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef`; only the *value* the c5c3 projection writes into that field changes. The path the issue specifies — `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db` — uses `cp.Name` (`controlplane`), not the projected `keystone.Name` (`controlplane-keystone`), which confirms ControlPlane-identity scoping owned by the c5c3 operator.

Concrete boundaries (what already exists → what this issue adds):

1. **Per-CR MariaDB resources already exist; the password source does not.** `mariaDBResourceKey` (`reconcile_database.go:70`) already scopes the `Database`/`User`/`Grant` CRs by `{keystone.Name, keystone.Namespace}`. `buildUser` (`reconcile_database.go:738`) takes the password from `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef` (key `password`). *This issue* makes that referenced Secret operator-owned and per-ControlPlane instead of the shared `keystone-db`.

2. **The static flat ExternalSecret exists; this issue removes it.** `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` reads `openstack/keystone/db` (properties `username`, `password`) into Secret `keystone-db` (namespace `openstack`), listed in `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml`. *This issue* deletes that file and its kustomization entry; the c5c3 operator generates a per-ControlPlane `ExternalSecret` in its place.

3. **The per-CR scoping pattern exists for three sibling credentials; this issue copies it for the DB credential.** Fernet/credential keys back up to `openstack/keystone/{ns}/{name}/fernet-keys` and `.../credential-keys` (`fernetKeysPushSecret`, `reconcile_fernet.go:437`/`:460`; `credentialKeysPushSecret`, `reconcile_credential.go:441`/`:464`). The Model-B admin password reads back per-CR from `bootstrap/{ns}/{name}/admin` and pushes there (`adminPasswordPushSecret`, `reconcile_passwordrotation.go:583`/`:604`). The admin Application Credential is scoped by `adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp) = openstack/keystone/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}/admin/app-credential` (`reconcile_korc.go:62`). *This issue* adds the DB-credential analog `openstack/keystone/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}/db`. (Note: the Model-B admin password path is keystone-name-scoped — `bootstrap/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}-keystone/admin`, `write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:158` — whereas the admin Application Credential and this new DB path are `cp.Name`-scoped; this issue follows the `cp.Name` form, per the layout above.) **Since CC-0114 (#412), an operator-created read-back `ExternalSecret` precedent for these per-CR paths also exists** — `ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret` (`reconcile_korc.go:1190`) — the direct structural template for the `dbCredentialExternalSecret(cp)` builder; note it uses the `external-secrets.io/v1` `ExternalSecret` type (`esov1`), **not** `v1alpha1`.

4. **The c5c3 ControlPlane already projects the Keystone DB spec wholesale.** `reconcile_keystone.go:113` does `keystone.Spec.Database = cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database`, which copies the `SecretRef`. Since **CC-0115** the value is no longer test-set: the defaulting webhook materializes `db.SecretRef.Name = DefaultDatabaseSecretName` (`"keystone-db"`) for any ControlPlane that omits it, in **both** modes (`controlplane_webhook.go:46`/`:122-124`, mode-neutral leaf). *This issue* overrides `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef` **in managed mode only** (`cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil`, the existing `dbManaged` predicate at `reconcile_infrastructure.go:89`) to the operator-owned Secret; the override happens during projection, so the inert `keystone-db` value the webhook stamped on `cp.Spec` is simply not consumed in managed mode. Brownfield ControlPlanes (Host-only, user-supplied DB) keep their `SecretRef` untouched — **but see boundary #10**: the CC-0115 `keystone-db` default now interacts with the deletion of the backing ExternalSecret.

5. **The bootstrap seeder already loops per ControlPlane; the DB seed is the one flat write left.** `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` seeds the Model-B admin password per `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` identity (`write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:150-170`; the K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml is **no longer seeded here** — CC-0114 moved it into the operator's `seedBootstrapCloudsYAML`), but still writes the flat `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db` (now **line 133**, using `password=${GENERATED_PASSWORD}` which is the `@generate` marker → a fresh random password per call). *This issue* moves the DB seed into the per-ControlPlane loop at `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{cp_ns}/{cp_name}/db` (`username=keystone`, `password=${GENERATED_PASSWORD}`; the loop already exposes `cp_ns`/`cp_name`) and removes the flat write — so each identity gets a distinct DB password.

6. **Read policy already covers the new path; only its comment is stale.** `eso-management.hcl` grants `read` on `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*` and `eso-control-plane.hcl` on `kv-v2/data/openstack/*`; both trailing-`*` grants already match `openstack/keystone/{ns}/{cp}/db`. *This issue* updates the `eso-management.hcl` `DEVIATION` comment, which currently cites the flat `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db`, to the per-CP shape (per the `Cross-reference access policies against all consumers` pattern — the new path must stay covered).

7. **The c5c3 operator already has the RBAC to generate ExternalSecrets.** `controlplane_controller.go:82` and `_helpers.tpl` (`c5c3-operator.rbacRules`, lines ~195-200) already grant `external-secrets.io/externalsecrets` and `core/secrets` `create;update;patch;delete`. *This issue* adds **no** RBAC; it adds a verifying Helm-render test so the in-sync rule is not silently dropped (`Sync RBAC markers with Helm ClusterRole templates`).

8. **The sub-reconciler + condition-coverage wiring exists; this issue registers one new sub-reconciler.** The ControlPlane `Reconcile` runs `Infrastructure → Keystone → KORC → AdminCredential → Catalog`, each wrapped in `instrumentSubReconciler` (`controlplane_controller.go:103-131`), with condition types listed in `subConditionTypes` (`controlplane_controller.go:57`) and `subReconcilerConditionTypes` (`instrumentation.go:40`). *This issue* inserts a `DBCredentials` sub-reconciler **before** `Keystone` (the credential must exist before the Keystone CR references it — `Validate reconcile step ordering matches data dependencies`) and registers its `DBCredentialsReady` condition in both maps so the CC-0089 drift guard and the `condition_type` metric label resolve.

9. **Standalone Keystone e2e fixtures depend on the flat seed; this issue re-points them.** Three suites create their own inline `ExternalSecret`s reading `openstack/keystone/db` for non-ControlPlane Keystone CRs: `tests/e2e/keystone/missing-secret/01-late-secrets.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/00-namespace.yaml` and its `chainsaw-test.yaml`. *This issue* re-points them to the seeded default identity path `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db` so dropping the flat seed does not strand them (`Check sibling files for same anti-pattern when PR improves a pattern`).

10. **CC-0115 defaults `secretRef.name` to `keystone-db` in brownfield mode too; deleting the backing secret leaves it dangling.** Before this issue, the `keystone-db` Secret was materialised cluster-wide by the static `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml`, so a brownfield-mode ControlPlane that omitted `secretRef` resolved its CC-0115 default `keystone-db` to a real Secret. *This issue* deletes that ExternalSecret (boundary #2) and does **not** create a per-CR DB Secret in brownfield mode (boundary #4), so a brownfield ControlPlane that relies on the `keystone-db` default would project a Keystone CR referencing a Secret that no longer exists in its child namespace. **Decision (this issue):** brownfield mode means *bring your own DB credentials* — a brownfield ControlPlane MUST supply `spec.infrastructure.database.secretRef` pointing at a user-provided Secret; the `keystone-db` default is documented as a managed-mode convenience name only. The CRD reference (`controlplane-crd.md`) must state this contract explicitly, and the standalone-Keystone `brownfield-database` e2e suite (which already sets `secretRef.name: keystone-db` and provisions its own Secret in `00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml`) is unaffected because it is a Keystone CR, not a ControlPlane, and never relied on the deleted ESO copy. (Leaving `DefaultDatabaseSecretName` itself unchanged is deliberate — narrowing the default to managed mode or renaming it is a separate webhook concern; see Non-Goals.)

Follow-up — dynamic DB secret engine (**#439**). The longer-term target is a dynamic per-tenant MariaDB secret engine (a role under `database/mariadb/creds/...` issuing short-lived, auto-revoked credentials) that replaces this issue's static KV secret entirely and shrinks the compromise window to the lease TTL. The per-ControlPlane, per-service path layout this issue establishes is the prerequisite that engine plugs into. That work — bootstrapping the engine into `deploy/`, the role/lease/revocation model, the operator's dynamic-credential consumption, and the migration off the static path — is tracked in **#439** and is **out of scope here** (neither designed nor implemented in this issue).

Considered and rejected: scoping by the Keystone CR identity (`keystone.Namespace/keystone.Name`) in the keystone operator, like the fernet/credential keys. It would also satisfy "two ControlPlanes never collide," but it contradicts the issue's explicit `{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}` layout and `feat(c5c3-operator)` framing, and would split DB-credential ownership away from the operator that already owns the ControlPlane-scoped admin credential.

Resolved design decision (the issue's "(and seed/PushSecret)" ambiguity): this issue makes OpenBao the source of truth via a **bootstrap seed + operator-generated read-back `ExternalSecret`** — the literal reading of "Replace the single static `keystone-db` `ExternalSecret` with an operator-generated, per-CR `ExternalSecret`," and the exact mirror of the Model-B admin *password* (a seeded, read-back credential), which unlike the admin Application Credential has no in-cluster minting step. The operator-generated `ExternalSecret` half now has a concrete precedent to copy verbatim — CC-0114's `ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret` (`reconcile_korc.go:1190`): `CreateOrUpdate` an `esov1.ExternalSecret` in `childNamespace(cp)`, `SecretStoreRef{Kind: "ClusterSecretStore", Name: openBaoClusterStoreName}`, owner-ref'd to the ControlPlane for GC. An operator-owned **backup/rotation `PushSecret`** and its `+/+` write policy (`push-keystone-db.hcl`, shape `kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/+/db`, mirroring `push-keystone-keys.hcl`) are deferred to the dynamic-engine follow-up (**#439**) and not created here, because a static seeded password has nothing to push — the dynamic engine supersedes that rotation path entirely.

**Motivation:**

Every Keystone today shares one DB credential source. Rotating or leaking `openstack/keystone/db` breaks or exposes every tenant's database at once — a single shared blast radius in a layout whose other per-CR credentials (admin password, K-ORC admin app credential, Fernet/credential keys) are already isolated by namespace and name. The OpenBao policies that protect those paths assume `+/+` per-CR shapes; the DB path has no equivalent, so the path-based tenant isolation the policy model depends on simply does not hold for DB credentials.

It also does not scale. Standing up a second ControlPlane reuses the same flat path, and the static `ExternalSecret` is hard-pinned to the default `openstack`/`controlplane` identity — the same deployment-shim limitation that, for the K-ORC admin clouds.yaml, CC-0114 (#412) **has already removed** by making the operator render that `ExternalSecret` per-ControlPlane (`ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret`). CC-0112 already moved the admin password and admin app credential onto per-ControlPlane paths; the static `keystone-db` `ExternalSecret` is now the last shared flat OpenStack-service secret in the inventory still pinned to that single identity, and leaving it flat means the multi-ControlPlane story is incomplete and every new service DB (Horizon next) would copy the flat anti-pattern.

Fixing it now is cheap and unblocks the harder target. The near-term change is mechanical: it reuses the per-ControlPlane scoping, the operator-owned secret pattern, and the `+/+` policy shape that CC-0110/CC-0112 already built. Doing it as a follow-up after more services exist means migrating N flat paths instead of one. The same change also establishes the per-ControlPlane, per-service path layout (`openstack/<service>/{ns}/{name}/db[/<dbname>]`) that the dynamic DB secret engine (**#439**) plugs into — without it, the dynamic engine would have no consistent tenant-scoped namespace to issue roles against.

**Affected Areas:**

- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_dbcredentials.go` (new): `reconcileDBCredentials(ctx, cp)` sub-reconciler; helpers `dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp) = "openstack/keystone/" + cp.Namespace + "/" + cp.Name + "/db"`, `dbCredentialSecretName(cp) = keystoneName(cp) + "-db-credentials"`, and a `dbCredentialExternalSecret(cp)` builder. In managed mode it ensures an `ExternalSecret` named `dbCredentialSecretName(cp)` in `childNamespace(cp)`, `secretStoreRef` `openbao-cluster-store` (`ClusterSecretStore`), reading `dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)` properties `username`/`password` into a Secret of the same name with keys `username`/`password`, owner-referenced to the ControlPlane; it sets `DBCredentialsReady` and gates on `WaitForExternalSecret`. In brownfield mode it is a no-op and `DBCredentialsReady=True`. Model `dbCredentialExternalSecret(cp)` on the existing `ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret` (`reconcile_korc.go:1190`, CC-0114): same `CreateOrUpdate` + child-namespace + `ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store` + controller-owner-ref shape, and the same `external-secrets.io/v1` `ExternalSecret` type (`esov1`) — **not** `esov1alpha1`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/controlplane_controller.go`: add `conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady = "DBCredentialsReady"`; append it to `subConditionTypes`; insert the `instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, "DBCredentials", …)` block between `Infrastructure` and `Keystone`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/instrumentation.go`: add `"DBCredentials": conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady` to `subReconcilerConditionTypes`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_keystone.go`: after `keystone.Spec.Database = cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database` (line 113), when `cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil`, set `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef = commonv1.SecretRefSpec{Name: dbCredentialSecretName(cp), Key: "password"}`. This supersedes the CC-0115 webhook default `DefaultDatabaseSecretName` (`"keystone-db"`) that the projection just copied from `cp.Spec` — the override is the only place the managed-mode value diverges from that default.
- `operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_webhook.go` (no code change, documentation/contract): `DefaultDatabaseSecretName = "keystone-db"` (`:46`) stays as-is — the value this issue's managed-mode projection override replaces. The webhook's brownfield default contract (boundary #10) is documented, not changed.
- `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` (delete) and `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml` (drop the `externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` entry).
- `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh`: remove the flat `write_secret_if_missing "kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db" …` (now **line 133**); inside the `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` loop (`:150-170`, alongside the existing `${admin_path}` seed) add `write_secret_if_missing "kv-v2/openstack/keystone/${cp_ns}/${cp_name}/db" "username=keystone" "password=${GENERATED_PASSWORD}"`.
- `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl`: rewrite the `DEVIATION` comment to cite `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/{namespace}/{name}/db` (the `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*` `read` grant is unchanged and already covers it); add a one-line note in `eso-control-plane.hcl` that `kv-v2/data/openstack/*` covers the per-CP DB path on the control-plane cluster.
- `tests/e2e/keystone/missing-secret/01-late-secrets.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/00-namespace.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/chainsaw-test.yaml`: re-point inline `ExternalSecret` `remoteRef.key` from `openstack/keystone/db` to `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db`; update the in-file comments.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go` (new) and updates to `reconcile_keystone_test.go`, `reconcile_infrastructure_test.go`, `integration_test.go`, `credential_invariant_test.go`, plus the `setupwithmanager_test.go`/condition-coverage drift-guard tests. **CC-0115 interactions in `integration_test.go`:** (a) the cp-level assertion `db.SecretRef.Name == DefaultDatabaseSecretName` (`:521-522`) stays valid (the override is on the *projected Keystone* CR, not `cp.Spec`), so add a *new* assertion that `TestIntegration_MinimalManagedToReady` projects a Keystone CR whose `spec.database.secretRef.name == controlplane-keystone-db-credentials`; (b) `driveControlPlaneToAdminCredentialReady` (`:~600`, which provisions deps at the `Default*` names) must also provision the operator-owned `controlplane-keystone-db-credentials` Secret, or the new `DBCredentialsReady` gate keeps the minimal-CR test from reaching `Ready=True`.
- `tests/e2e/keystone/db-credential-scoping/` (new Chainsaw suite) per the one-feature-one-E2E-directory convention.
- `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl` test coverage: add/extend a Helm render assertion that the `external-secrets.io/externalsecrets` create/update rule is present (no rule change).
- Docs: `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-reconciler.md` (new owned-resource row + RemoteKey + the `DBCredentials` sub-reconciler/condition), `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md` (managed-mode `SecretRef` is operator-owned; **and** the brownfield contract from boundary #10 — a brownfield ControlPlane must supply `database.secretRef`, the CC-0115 `keystone-db` default is managed-mode convenience only), `docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md` and `docs/reference/testing/keystone-e2e-tests.md` (replace `openstack/keystone/db` with the per-CP path), and the architecture submodule `architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md` (per-CP DB path in the inventory; the dynamic-engine target is documented in **#439**, not here).

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Applying a managed-mode ControlPlane `controlplane` in namespace `openstack` produces an `ExternalSecret` and Secret both named `controlplane-keystone-db-credentials` in `openstack`, the `ExternalSecret`'s `remoteRef.key` is `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db`, and the materialised Secret has keys `username` and `password` — asserted by a c5c3 envtest/integration test and the new Chainsaw suite.
- [ ] The projected Keystone CR's `spec.database.secretRef.name` equals `controlplane-keystone-db-credentials` (not the CC-0115 `DefaultDatabaseSecretName` default `keystone-db`) in managed mode — asserted in `reconcile_keystone_test.go` and in the CC-0115 minimal-CR path (`TestIntegration_MinimalManagedToReady`), which reaches `Ready=True` with the new `DBCredentials` gate satisfied. The cp-level `cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.SecretRef.Name` stays `keystone-db` (the override lives in the projection), so the existing `integration_test.go` defaulting assertion is unchanged.
- [ ] Two ControlPlanes — same name in different namespaces, and different names in one namespace — yield distinct `dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor` values and distinct Secret names; a table test asserts no pair collides.
- [ ] A brownfield ControlPlane (`spec.infrastructure.database.host` set, `clusterRef` nil) creates **no** DB-credential `ExternalSecret`, leaves `spec.database.secretRef` as supplied, and reports `DBCredentialsReady=True` — asserted in `reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go`. The CRD reference documents (boundary #10) that a brownfield ControlPlane must supply its own `secretRef`; the CC-0115 `keystone-db` default no longer resolves to a cluster Secret once the static ExternalSecret is gone.
- [ ] `grep -R "openstack/keystone/db\b"` over `deploy/` and `tests/` returns no flat-path reference; `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` is gone and absent from `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml`. (This targets the OpenBao **path** `openstack/keystone/db`; the Kubernetes Secret-name constant `DefaultDatabaseSecretName = "keystone-db"` in `controlplane_webhook.go` is a different string, is intentionally retained, and is correctly not matched by this grep.)
- [ ] `write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` seeds `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/<ns>/<cp>/db` once per `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` identity and no longer writes `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db`; the default `openstack/controlplane` identity yields `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db`.
- [ ] `DBCredentialsReady` is a member of both `subConditionTypes` and `subReconcilerConditionTypes`, the existing CC-0089 drift-guard tests pass, and the ControlPlane `Ready` condition is `True` only when `DBCredentialsReady` is also `True`.
- [ ] `reconcileDBCredentials` runs before `reconcileKeystone`; an integration test confirms the Keystone CR is not created until the DB-credential Secret exists (no `WaitingForDBCredentials` flap on first reconcile of a managed ControlPlane).
- [ ] `helm template` of the c5c3 chart renders the `external-secrets.io/externalsecrets` `create`/`update` rule in the ClusterRole, asserted by a Helm test.
- [ ] The re-pointed keystone e2e suites (`missing-secret`, `pod-security-restricted`) pass against a cluster seeded only with the per-CP path; the brownfield-database suite is unaffected.
- [ ] The architecture and `docs/reference` pages describe the per-ControlPlane DB path `openstack/keystone/{ns}/{name}/db` and note the reserved multi-DB form `…/db/<dbname>`; no doc still references `openstack/keystone/db`. (The dynamic-engine target design is documented under #439, not here.)

**Non-Goals:**

- Operator-owned backup/rotation **PushSecret** for the DB credential and its `push-keystone-db.hcl` `+/+` write policy (`kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/+/db`): deferred to **#439** (a static seeded password has nothing to push, and the dynamic engine supersedes that rotation path).
- Implementing the multi-DB-per-ControlPlane leaf `openstack/<service>/{ns}/{name}/db/<dbname>`: the layout and policy globs are chosen to accommodate it, but Keystone needs exactly one DB, so no `<dbname>` parameter is added now (no speculative scope, `YAGNI`).
- Designing **or** implementing the dynamic DB secret engine (`database/mariadb/creds/...`) or bootstrapping it into `deploy/`: split out to **#439**; this issue neither designs nor wires it.
- The single-ESO-identity / `ClusterSecretStore` confused-deputy problem (per-tenant OpenBao identities via templated policies + namespaced `SecretStore`): separate issue.
- The `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret (#412): CC-0114 **already** made the per-ControlPlane K-ORC admin clouds.yaml `ExternalSecret` operator-created (`ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret`); what remains in `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml` is only the static `orc-system` global-default copy (a parallel concern), so no further templating work is in scope here — that file serves as the read-back-`ExternalSecret` precedent this issue copies, not a target.
- The `infrastructure/mariadb` root password (`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/mariadb-root-password.yaml`), still flat and shared: an infrastructure credential, not a per-ControlPlane service credential; out of scope.
- Changing the Keystone operator's DB-consumption code (`buildUser`, `reconcileDBConnectionSecret`, `reconcileSecrets`): it consumes `spec.database.secretRef` unchanged; only the projected value changes.
- Changing the CC-0115 defaulting webhook (`controlplane_webhook.go`): `DefaultDatabaseSecretName = "keystone-db"` stays as-is and stays mode-neutral. Narrowing it to managed mode, renaming it, or teaching the webhook to emit the per-CR `dbCredentialSecretName(cp)` is out of scope — this issue keeps the value-change in the projection (boundary #4) and only *documents* the brownfield contract (boundary #10).

**References:**

- Issue #435 — feat(c5c3-operator): scope service database credentials per ControlPlane and per database.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go` — `mariaDBResourceKey:70`, `buildUser:738`, `buildDatabase`, `buildGrant`.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go` — `reconcileDBConnectionSecret`; reads `username`/`password` from `spec.database.secretRef`.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go` — `reconcileSecrets`; `openBaoClusterStoreName`, `WaitForExternalSecret`.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:437/460`, `reconcile_credential.go:441/464`, `reconcile_passwordrotation.go:583/604` — per-CR `RemoteKey` precedents and `DeletionPolicy` choices.

requirements.description: The c5c3 operator SHALL, in managed mode (cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil), CreateOrUpdate an external-secrets.io/v1 ExternalSecret named dbCredentialSecretName(cp) in childNamespace(cp) that reads dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp) properties 'username' and 'password' from ClusterSecretStore 'openbao-cluster-store' into a Secret of the same name, owner-referenced to the ControlPlane.
requirements.rationale: Replaces the single shared static keystone-db ExternalSecret with a per-ControlPlane operator-owned one, mirroring CC-0114's ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret, so DB credentials gain the per-CR isolation the rest of the secret layout already has.

implementation_notes:
FEATURE ID: CC-0116 (issue #435). This is a Go/Kubernetes-operator feature — there is no frontend/hooks layer; the 'backend' is the c5c3 controller-runtime reconciler and its deploy/test/docs surface.

SCOPE (this issue, formerly 'stage a'): per-ControlPlane STATIC KV DB credential via a bootstrap seed + operator-generated read-back ExternalSecret. OUT OF SCOPE / deferred to #439: the dynamic MariaDB secret engine, the operator-owned backup/rotation PushSecret and its push-keystone-db.hcl +/+ policy, and the multi-DB-per-ControlPlane leaf '.../db/<dbname>' (layout reserved, not parameterised — Keystone needs exactly one DB).

ARCHITECTURE (component relationships):

  write-bootstrap-secrets.sh  --seeds-->  OpenBao kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{ns}/{cp}/db  (username,password)
        (per KORC_CONTROLPLANES identity, @generate password)            ^
                                                                         | reads (ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store)
  ControlPlane(managed) --Reconcile--> [Infrastructure] -> [DBCredentials] -> [Keystone] -> [KORC] -> [AdminCredential] -> [Catalog]
                                                 |                |                 |
                                                 |   CreateOrUpdate esov1.ExternalSecret 'controlplane-keystone-db-credentials'
                                                 |   (owner-ref=ControlPlane, target.creationPolicy=Owner)
                                                 |                |
                                                 |        ESO materialises --> Secret 'controlplane-keystone-db-credentials' (keys username,password)
                                                 |                |                 ^
                                                 |   WaitForExternalSecret gates DBCredentialsReady
                                                 v                                  |
                                          projects Keystone CR; spec.database.secretRef = {controlplane-keystone-db-credentials, password}
                                                                                    |
  Keystone operator (UNCHANGED) buildUser / reconcileDBConnectionSecret read username/password from that Secret

DATA FLOW: identity -> seeded OpenBao path (random password) -> ExternalSecret (operator-built, per-CP) -> materialised Secret -> projected keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef -> MariaDB User CR + connection string. Managed mode is the only path that overrides the projected SecretRef; brownfield short-circuits before any of this.

KEY PRECEDENT TO COPY VERBATIM: ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret (operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go:1204-1237, CC-0114). Same esov1 'github.com/external-secrets/external-secrets/apis/externalsecrets/v1' type, same {ClusterSecretStore, openBaoClusterStoreName} secretStoreRef, same CreateOrUpdate-with-SetControllerReference-in-closure shape, same childNamespace(cp) target. The only differences: two Data entries (username,password) instead of one (clouds.yaml), and remoteRef.key = dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp).

GOTCHAS (from research): (1) SetControllerReference MUST be inside the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure, never on a pre-populated object, or CreateOrUpdate sees a spurious mismatch. (2) WaitForExternalSecret (internal/common/secrets/secrets.go:44) returns (false,nil) for not-found — treat as 'not ready, requeue', not an error. (3) target.creationPolicy=Owner so the materialised Secret is GC'd with the ExternalSecret/ControlPlane. (4) envtest has NO real ESO controller — integration tests must call simulators.SimulateExternalSecretSync after the operator creates the ExternalSecret, or the gate hangs forever; driveControlPlaneToAdminCredentialReady must simulate the DB-credential ExternalSecret BEFORE Keystone since DBCredentials now precedes Keystone. (5) the webhook default is mode-neutral and shared with the keystone operator (controlplane_webhook.go:39-41 comment) — do NOT touch DefaultDatabaseSecretName; the value-change lives only in the projection (boundary #4). (6) The Helm rule-count assertion is lengthEqual 15 — adding a 'contains' assert must not change the count.

DECISIONS:
D1 (ordering): The issue is explicit that DBCredentials runs BEFORE Keystone (between Infrastructure and Keystone), because the Keystone CR references the DB Secret. One research note suggested 'between Keystone and KORC' — that is incorrect for this issue; follow the issue. subConditionTypes order: Infrastructure, DBCredentials, Keystone, KORC, AdminCredential, Catalog.
D2 (new e2e suite location): The issue text says tests/e2e/keystone/db-credential-scoping/, but research confirms ControlPlane-CR e2e suites live under tests/e2e/c5c3/ (e.g. full-controlplane-keystone, multi-controlplane) while tests/e2e/keystone/ holds Keystone-CR suites. Acceptance criterion #1 asserts ControlPlane behavior (applying a ControlPlane produces the ExternalSecret), so the suite is placed under tests/e2e/c5c3/db-credential-scoping/ following full-controlplane-keystone (presence-guarded, SKIPs when the c5c3+K-ORC stack is absent). Reviewer: confirm this directory choice.
D3 (bootstrap seed marking): mirror the existing admin_path seed invocation exactly. If that seed calls mark_eso_managed, do the same for the DB seed for consistency; if not, omit it — a static seeded password has no PushSecret to overwrite in this issue (rotation deferred to #439).

DEFERRED ITEMS (explicit, with rationale and timing):
- Architecture submodule doc architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md: the per-CP DB path inventory belongs here, BUT architecture/ is a git submodule (url C5C3/C5C3) and is READ-ONLY from this worktree (core rule 12 — no submodule modifications). Rationale: cross-repo write would create inconsistent state. Timing: open a follow-up PR against C5C3/C5C3 (or use the sync-forge-docs skill) after this lands. Do NOT edit any path under architecture/ here.
- Operator-owned backup/rotation PushSecret + push-keystone-db.hcl (+/+ kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/+/db): deferred to #439. Rationale: a static seeded password has nothing to push; the dynamic engine supersedes that rotation path. Timing: with #439.
- Dynamic MariaDB secret engine (database/mariadb/creds/...): designed and implemented in #439. Rationale: longer-term target; this issue establishes the per-CP path layout it plugs into. Timing: #439.
- Multi-DB-per-ControlPlane leaf '.../db/<dbname>': layout/policy globs accommodate it but no <dbname> parameter is added (YAGNI — Keystone needs one DB). Timing: when a second service DB per ControlPlane is introduced.
- Narrowing/renaming DefaultDatabaseSecretName or making the webhook emit the per-CR name: separate webhook concern, out of scope (Non-Goals). Timing: a future webhook issue if desired.

SECURITY/OBSERVABILITY checkpoints: per-CR path scoping is the security control (blast-radius confinement); the DBCredentialsReady condition + the instrumentSubReconciler duration/error metrics (condition_type label) provide observability; no new RBAC is granted (existing externalsecrets/secrets create-update-patch-delete already present), verified by the Helm render test.
